normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem spec_sort_multiset (xs : List Int) : Multiset.ofList (sort xs) = Multiset.ofList xs
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The output contains exactly the same elements as the input, counting multiplicities (multiset preservation).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem spec_sort_sorted (xs : List Int) : Sorted (· ≤ ·) (sort xs)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The output list is sorted in nondecreasing order.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem spec_sort_length (xs : List Int) : (sort xs).length = xs.length
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The output has the same length as the input (follows from multiset preservation, but can be required explicitly).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem spec_sort_perm (xs : List Int) : xs ~p sort xs
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The output is a permutation of the input (permutation equivalence induced by multiset equality).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem impl_correctness (xs : List Int) : Multiset.ofList (sort xs) = Multiset.ofList xs ∧ Sorted (· ≤ ·) (sort xs)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Combines the two main correctness properties: multiset preservation and sortedness.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem impl_terminates (xs : List Int) : ∃ n : Nat, sort xs = sortAux n xs
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The implementation terminates / is well-founded (expressed via an auxiliary bounded recursion or well-founded measure).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem impl_pure (xs : List Int) : sort xs = sort xs
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The implementation is deterministic/pure with respect to the input (no hidden state); useful when mapping from Rust to Lean.
